text: "A rival faction is using an artifact to gain an economic advantage. I need an independent force to 'claim' it and to bring it back to me."

script:
$missionId = sep_aim
SetTextHighlightColors h $sep_aim_marketFactionColor $sep_aim_marketFactionColor h h h
SetTextHighlights $sep_aim_artifactName $sep_aim_marketName $sep_aim_marketFactionName $sep_aim_marketSystem $sep_aim_marines $sep_aim_reward
Call $sep_aim_ref showMap "Target: $sep_aim_marketName"
text:
"An artifact called $sep_aim_artifactName is undermining the $personFaction's economic standing. I need an independent force that can take the artifact and place it under $personFaction control," $heOrShe says as a mission briefing appears on your screen.

"The artifact is on $sep_aim_marketName, a $sep_aim_marketFactionName colony in the $sep_aim_marketSystem. You'll need roughly $sep_aim_marines marines to secure it." $HeOrShe leans forward slightly. "If successful, I am authorized to offer $sep_aim_reward credits, along with a sizable boost to your reputation. Interested?"
A $manOrWoman sits at the counter, juggling two tripads with an annoyed expression. The untouched drink beside them reflects the glow of the screens.

options: sep_aim_startBar:Approach the $manOrWoman with an annoyed expression

script:
$missionId = sep_aim
SetTextHighlightColors h $sep_aim_marketFactionColor $sep_aim_marketFactionColor h h h
SetTextHighlights $sep_aim_artifactName $sep_aim_marketName $sep_aim_marketFactionName $sep_aim_marketSystem $sep_aim_marines $sep_aim_reward
Call $sep_aim_ref showMap "Target: $sep_aim_marketName"
text:
$HeOrShe looks at you with annoyance before softening $hisOrHer gaze. "A Captain, I presume?" You nod in response. "Good," $heOrShe says. "I have an issue that might interest you." $HeOrShe slides a tripad toward you, "If you're interested, take a look."

On the tripad, you see details of economic issues caused by an artifact called $sep_aim_artifactName. Reading further, you find that the $personFaction has authorized the seizure of the artifact. Its location revealed to be on $sep_aim_marketName, a $sep_aim_marketFactionName colony in the $sep_aim_marketSystem, along with an outline of the colony's defenses estimating that around $sep_aim_marines marines would be needed to secure it. At the end, a listed reward shows $sep_aim_reward credits.

The $manOrWoman coughs, pulling your attention. "So, Captain, are you in?"

The raid is a success. Your marines push through fortified defenses with precision, suffering a few casualties but managing to overcome the resistance.

They secure the artifact and move it under heavy guard, transporting it aboard a shuttle before returning safely to your ship.

script:
ShowPersonVisual
SEPDoHasCargoCheck $sep_aim_artifactId 1 sep_aim_giveArtifact
SetTooltip sep_aim_giveArtifact "Need $sep_aim_artifactName in cargo."
SetTooltipHighlights sep_aim_giveArtifact $sep_aim_artifactName
SetStoryOption sep_aim_keepArtifact 2 0 general "keep artifact"
text:
"Captain? I didn’t expect you back so soon," $heOrShe says, a flicker of surprise crossing $hisOrHer face. "I only heard that the raid on the target had started, but I didn’t know it would be finished so quickly."

$HeOrShe leans forward slightly, eyes narrowing with intent. "Now, the artifact. Hand it over so I can put it to good use, and you can get your reward."
options:
sep_aim_keepArtifact:Convince to keep the artifact
sep_aim_giveArtifact:Deliver the artifact
cutCommLink:Go back and think about your decision

text:
Your security officer delivers the artifact, and $heOrShe is immediately notified of its arrival. "Well done, Captain," $heOrShe says, a hint of relief in $hisOrHer voice.

"With this, my economic issues are solved while my influence grows stronger. Thank you, Captain," $heOrShe adds, giving you a nod of gratitude.

$HeOrShe folds $hisOrHer arms, weighing the decision before finally speaking. "Alright, Captain, if keeping the artifact can benefit the $personFaction's interests, I’ll allow it."

$HeOrShe’s tone hardens. "However, the agreed-upon reward stays with me. Take it as a trade-off. You have what you wanted, and I keep my terms intact."
“Here's one. A researcher is looking for a fleet to gather telemetry on hyperspace anomalies. May take some time but the pay is good.”

$HeOrShe scrolls through $hisOrHer tripad, searching for the mission entry.

"Here it is," $heOrShe says. "The researcher needs about $sep_harm_dataInDays days’ worth of hyperspace anomaly data. They're providing a few researchers and some equipment to assist. All you need to do is locate the anomalies and let the team handle the rest."

$HeOrShe continues, transmitting the full mission briefing. "There’s no set deadline for this assignment. Just gather the required data and return it here. Once done, the researcher will award you $sep_harm_reward. Interested?"

You skim through the finer details of the mission. According to the researcher, hyperspace anomalies often occur near the fringes of the sector and often attracted to high-energy waves, like those emitted by sensor bursts.
You notice a table filled with research papers and a $personPost frantically rearranging them.

"This isn't enough," $heOrShe grumbles. "I need something more, more..." $HeOrShe looks at you curiously. "...accurate." $HeOrShe pauses.

"A captain?" $HeOrShe asks. You nod in response. "Great! You're just in time. I need $sep_harm_dataInDays days’ worth of hyperspace anomaly data. If you haven’t encountered them before, hyperspace anomalies often occur near the fringes of the sector and often attracted to high-energy waves, like those emitted by sensor bursts," $heOrShe continues.

"I'll provide a few researchers and some equipment to assist. All you need to do is locate the anomalies and let the team handle the rest." $HeOrShe hands you a tripad with a hastily written contract. "There’s no set deadline for this assignment. Just gather the required data and deliver it to me. Once done, I’ll pay you $sep_harm_reward. Interested?"

You transmit the completed hyperspace anomaly data to the $personPost.

$HeOrShe thanks you for the data before sending you your reward.

You transmit the completed hyperspace anomaly data to the $personPost.

$HeOrShe thanks you for the data before getting lost in its details, even forgeting to cut the comm link.
"A trader spent all their credits on commodities and forgot to hire protection, now they are looking for a quick hired gun to escort their trade fleet."

options: sep_fem_start:Ask about the trader looking for a quick hired gun

script:
SetTextHighlightColors h h h h
SetTextHighlights $sep_fem_itemName $sep_fem_mrktName $sep_fem_sysName $sep_fem_creditReward
Call $sep_fem_ref showMap "Target: $sep_fem_sysName"
text:
"A trader bought a supply of $sep_fem_itemName and forgot to hire combat ships to protect their shipment," $heOrShe explains. "They need a combat capable fleet, to escort them all the way to $sep_fem_mrktName in the $sep_fem_sysName."

$HeOrShe adds, "It's a one way trip. You go there, wait for the delivery to finish, and your job is done."

$HeOrShe continues. "In exchange, the trader is willing to split the profits seventy thirty, which comes out to $sep_fem_creditReward. Interested?"
An anxious $manOrWoman with $hisOrHer hand on $hisOrHer temple is looking at trade papers with concern.

options: sep_fem_startBar:Approach the $manOrWoman looking at trade papers with corncern

script:
SetTextHighlightColors h h h h
SetTextHighlights $sep_fem_itemName $sep_fem_mrktName $sep_fem_sysName $sep_fem_creditReward
Call $sep_fem_ref showMap "Target: $sep_fem_sysName"
text:
The $manOrWoman sizes you up with a quick glance, then checks their datapad. Their expression shifts, as if something they see there confirms a good impression.

They lean in a little. "I picked up a surplus of $sep_fem_itemName, but made the mistake of not hiring any combat escorts. My transports are supposed to head to $sep_fem_mrktName in the $sep_fem_sysName, but let’s just say fleets like mine tend to get noticed."

They swirl the drink in their glass. "I need the right kind of fleet to escort them and get there without trouble. In exchange, I am willing to split the profits seventy thirty, which comes out to $sep_fem_creditReward." $HeOrShe says, slowly sliding a contract toward you. "Interested?"
"This one is interesting. A medical transport needs an escort. It's headed to a colony that might be dealing with some kind of virus, and a cure was manufactured here."

options: sep_fem_start:Ask about escorting a medical transport headed to a colony

script:
SetTextHighlightColors h h h
SetTextHighlights $sep_fem_mrktName $sep_fem_sysName $sep_fem_creditReward
Call $sep_fem_ref showMap "Target: $sep_fem_sysName"
text:
$HeOrShe taps $hisOrHer tripad. "We have a medical transport scheduled to depart, carrying what is officially listed as a cure for a virus." $HisOrHer tone seemingly questionable. "It needs an escort to $sep_fem_mrktName in the $sep_fem_sysName, then back here, a round trip, curiously enough."

$HeOrShe continues. "Anyways, once the escort is completed, a hefty $sep_fem_creditReward payment has been authorized by fleet intel. Interested?"
In the dark corner of the bar, a tripad shines on the face of a $manOrWoman, $hisOrHer eyes glances at you.

options: sep_fem_startBar:Approach the $manOrWoman in the dark corner of the bar staring at you

script:
SetTextHighlightColors h h h
SetTextHighlights $sep_fem_mrktName $sep_fem_sysName $sep_fem_creditReward
Call $sep_fem_ref showMap "Target: $sep_fem_sysName"
text:
The $manOrWoman looks up, then hands you the tripad $heOrShe is holding. "A medical transport needs an escort. It's carrying enough produce to keep people very happy," $HeOrShe says, watching your reaction.

"The fleet needs to reach $sep_fem_mrktName in the $sep_fem_sysName. Once there, wait for the transport to finish its... delivery, then escort it back here." $HeOrShe pauses.

"If you can keep the fleet safe for the entire trip, I'll pay you $sep_fem_creditReward." $HeOrShe clasps $hisOrHer hands. "Interested?"
"An uprising is brewing on a competitor's colony. Fleet intel wants to pour a bit of oil on the fire and needs someone to guide the way."

options: sep_fem_start:Ask about the uprising brewing on a competitor's colony

script:
SetTextHighlightColors h h h
SetTextHighlights $sep_fem_mrktName $sep_fem_sysName $sep_fem_creditReward
Call $sep_fem_ref showMap "Target: $sep_fem_sysName"
text:
"Tensions are rising on $sep_fem_mrktName in the $sep_fem_sysName. Certain groups are now quite motivated." $HeOrShe taps a few notes on the tripad. "A transport fleet has been dispatched to provide supplies that might help things along."

$HeOrShe meets your eyes. "Your task is simple. Escort the fleet to its destination and make sure it survives long enough to offload its cargo. After that, your involvement ends."

$HeOrShe continues, "Do that, and I'll authorize a payment of $sep_fem_creditReward, along with a favorable report to the officials of the $Faction. Interested?"
While scanning the bar, you notice a lone tripad on a table and a ringing call coming from it.

options: sep_fem_startBar:Answer the lone tripad with a ringing call coming from it

script:
SetTextHighlightColors h h h
SetTextHighlights $sep_fem_mrktName $sep_fem_sysName $sep_fem_creditReward
Call $sep_fem_ref showMap "Target: $sep_fem_sysName"
text:
The face on the screen regards you with mild surprise, then nods as if recalculating a plan.

"l'll keep this brief. A transport fleet is preparing to depart, carrying... supplies intended to encourage certain movements on a competitor's colony. Nothing you need to involve yourself in beyond the escort."

$HeOrShe sends you a location. "Guide the fleet safely to $sep_fem_mrktName in the $sep_fem_sysName, ensure the cargo is delivered, and your role is done. In return, you'll receive $sep_fem_creditReward and a favorable note to who I work with."

$HeOrShe pauses. "Are you in... or out?"
"An envoy needs to mediate issues on another colony. Their fleet needs a military escort to guide and protect them to their desitination."

options: sep_fem_start:Ask about escorting an envoy to another colony

script:
SetTextHighlightColors h h h
SetTextHighlights $sep_fem_mrktName $sep_fem_sysName $sep_fem_creditReward
Call $sep_fem_ref showMap "Target: $sep_fem_sysName"
text:
$HeOrShe adjusts $hisOrHer posture before speaking. "An envoy needs to mediate some issues on $sep_fem_mrktName in the $sep_fem_sysName. The colony refuses any official $Faction military escorts, so we need a fleet that just happens to be heading in the same direction."

$HeOrShe glances $hisOrHer datapad, then back at you. "Your job is to accompany the envoy's fleet to the colony, wait for them to finish their assignment, and then escort them back here. Nothing more complicated than that."

$HeOrShe clasps $hisOrHer hands. "Once the envoy returns safely, you'll receive $sep_fem_creditReward. So, what do you say?"
You noticed a well spoken $manOrWoman converses with people within the bar as if probing them for something.

options: sep_fem_startBar:Sit beside the $manOrWoman probing info from bar customers

script:
SetTextHighlightColors h h h
SetTextHighlights $sep_fem_mrktName $sep_fem_sysName $sep_fem_creditReward
Call $sep_fem_ref showMap "Target: $sep_fem_sysName"
text:
The $manOrWoman starts a casual conversation with you about various events in the sector. After a moment, $heOrShe gives you an evaluating look. "Captain," $HeOrShe says with certainty, "I believe you might just be the one I'm looking for."

$HeOrShe leans in slightly. "Relations between the $Faction and the colony on $sep_fem_mrktName in the $sep_fem_sysName are delicate. I’ve been assigned to mediate, but the colony refuses any official military escort. That means we need an alternative, and your fleet fits the requirements."

$HeOrShe continues. "If you're interested, my superiors are prepared to pay $sep_fem_creditReward for a safe round trip to the colony and back."

text: "A ranking official has lost someone important and is offering a substantial credit reward for their rescue or recovery."

options: sep_sar_start:Ask about rescuing or recovering someone important

text:
"$sep_sar_survivorFullName, a close relative of the official, was part of a patrol fleet that was due to return over a week ago, but the fleet never arrived. Fleet intel has marked the fleet as 'lost,' but the official refuses to accept that and insists their relative is still alive."

$HeOrShe swipes upward on $hisOrHer tripad, sending a shared intel briefing to you. "The fleet's last known heading was toward $sep_sar_possibleLoc, after which they never returned."

$HeOrShe leans back slightly. "Your task is to search the area and recover the relative, dead or alive. If successful, the official is offering $sep_sar_creditReward. Interested?"
A gloomy $manOrWoman sits hunched over the bar, clutching a faded photo in $hisOrHer hands. $HisOrHer expression heavy with grief and stubborn hope.

options: sep_sar_startBar:Approach the gloomy $manOrWoman and ask if $heOrShe's alright

text:
$HeOrShe glances at you, suspicious at first, then looks back at the photo with a heavy sigh. "$sep_sar_survivorFullName, my close relative, was part of a patrol fleet that was due to return over a week ago, but the fleet never arrived. Fleet intel has marked the fleet as 'lost,' but I can't accept that."

$HeOrShe grips the photo and continues. "When I last spoke to $sep_sar_survivorFirstName, $sep_sar_survivorHeOrShe mentioned going toward $sep_sar_possibleLoc, after which $sep_sar_survivorHeOrShe never returned." 

$HeOrShe sets the photo down on the table and meets your gaze. "You look like a capable captain, if you're willing to take the risk, I'll pay $sep_sar_creditReward for $sep_sar_survivorFirstName's safe return."

Your $shipOrFleet approaches $aOrAn $nameInText.

A preliminary scan detects faint bio-signatures scattered throughout the hull.

Your comms officer promptly broadcasts an emergency hail but receives no response. Without hesitation, you direct your chief salvage operator and chief medical officer to initiate a rescue operation.

A moment later, your officers find survivors scattered throughout the hull, including your rescue target, $sep_sar_survivorFullName. Luckily, you arrived before they ran out of oxygen. Most are alive, including the rescue target.

According to the survivors, their fleet was ambushed after responding to a distress signal. Their ship managed to escape but was disabled, lowering its sensor profile and rendering it undetectable.

Shortly after, your officers return and escort the injured survivors to the infirmary.

A moment later, your officers find survivors scattered throughout the hull, including your rescue target, $sep_sar_survivorFullName. Unfortunately, they ran out of oxygen, and all have died, including the rescue target.

Recovered logs reveal that the ship's fleet was ambushed after responding to a distress signal. The ship managed to escape but was disabled, lowering its sensor profile and rendering it undetectable.

Shortly after, your officers return and store the remains in your cargo holds.
"A concerned official is worried about a close friend. They want you to find this person and bring them back if possible."

options: sep_sar_start:Ask about the offical worried about a friend

text:
"$sep_sar_survivorFullName, a close friend of the official, was sent to survey $sep_sar_possibleLoc," $heOrShe says. "So far, the mission hasn’t reported any problems, and the estimated return is still nearly three weeks away. But the official is worried that something might have gone wrong."

$HeOrShe pauses, lowering $hisOrHer datapad. "Normally, we’d just wait, but the official is adamant about sending someone to find or assist their friend," $heOrShe continues. "They’re even offering $sep_sar_creditReward to anyone willing to go. So... are you interested?"
You notice a worried $manOrWoman tapping $hisOrHer glass of alchohol while looking at a mission manifest.

The worried $manOrWoman looks up, giving you a quick assessing glance before deciding to speak.

"$sep_sar_survivorFullName, a close friend of mine," $heOrShe says while giving you a mission manifest, "was sent to survey $sep_sar_possibleLoc. It's a simple job, but a few days ago I had this dreadful feeling, and it has only grown since."

$HeOrShe continues, "I know the mission is still far from its return date, but I can't ignore this any longer." $HeOrShe looks to you with quiet urgency. "Please, Captain. Rescue my friend, and I'll pay you $sep_sar_creditReward for your help."

Your $shipOrFleet approaches $entityName.

A preliminary scan detects a makeshift outpost with faint bio-signatures inside.

Your comms officer promptly broadcasts emergency hails and stands by for any response.

Your comms crackle to life, and a weak voice breaks through. "Thank God," they whisper, the voice trailing off before a heavy thud echoes over the channel. Your comms officer tries to respond, but silence follows. You waste no time and order your medical and military teams to initiate a rescue operation.

As the operation unfolds, one of your officers reports the cause of the group’s predicament. Their fleet had been attacked in orbit, leaving the group on the surface stranded without support.

Afterward, the survivors are brought aboard. Shaken and exhausted, they express deep relief, especially $sep_sar_survivorFullName, who cannot believe help arrived so quickly.

Your comms pick up only static as repeated emergency hails go unanswered. With no response from the outpost, you deploy your medical and military teams to investigate.

The teams soon report a grim scene. Inside the outpost, they find only malnourished and oxygen-deprived bodies, among them $sep_sar_survivorFullName, slumped over a console, still hoping for help. Recovered logs reveal that a survey fleet had been attacked in orbit, leaving the group on the surface stranded without support.

Afterward, the remains are brought aboard. Though none survived, your crew ensures every fallen explorer is prepared to be returned home for proper honors.
"An associate of a ranking official was kidnapped and has filed a formal request to rescue said individual."

options: sep_sar_start:Ask about rescuing an associate of ranking offical

script:
SetTextHighlightColors h h h h
SetTextHighlights $sep_sar_survivorFullName $sep_sar_creditRansom $sep_sar_possibleLoc $sep_sar_creditReward
text:
"A pirate terror group raided one of our cargo stations," $heOrShe begins. "They took several hostages, including our associate, $sep_sar_survivorFullName."

$HeOrShe slides a datapad across the table. "They’re demanding $sep_sar_creditRansom and want the exchange to happen in $sep_sar_possibleLoc."

$HeOrShe leans back slightly. "Your task is to negotiate with them if you can, and return with the hostages."

Then, with a calm nod, $heOrShe adds, "Once the job is done, I’m authorized to award you $sep_sar_creditReward. Interested?"
A reserve $manOrWoman sits alone, quiet and distant, carrying a subtle air of concern.

options: sep_sar_startBar:Approach the reserve $manOrWoman carrying a subtle air of concern

script:
SetTextHighlightColors h h h h

text:
The $manOrWoman glances you over, eyes briefly scanning your attire and ship insignia. After a quick tap at their datapad, they lean forward.

"An associate of mine, $sep_sar_survivorFullName, was taken hostage during a routine cargo transfer. Soon after, the kidnappers demanded $sep_sar_creditRansom," $heOrShe explains, sliding a datapad toward you.

"The kidnappers want the exchange to happen somewhere in $sep_sar_possibleLoc," $heOrShe points at a map on the datapad. "You can negotiate or use force. What matters is that my associate is... safe," $heOrShe implies.

"Once my associate is back at my side, I'll award you $sep_sar_creditReward," $heOrShe meets your gaze. "What do you think?"

Your screen crackles to life, a grainy image of a hard-eyed officer fills the screen.

"So... you must be the ones carrying the ransom." $HisOrHer voice drips with suspicion as $heOrShe sizes up your $shipOrFleet.

"We made it clear. $sep_sar_creditRansom credits, transferred clean and quiet. No tricks, no games. Hand it over, and maybe we all can walk away alive." The channel hangs open, the kidnappers clearly waiting for your next move.

"An associate's nephew is currently being held in a pirate hideout and wants to personally fund a rescue mission. I am currently the appointed liason, so if your hearing this, you're my first choice."

options: sep_sar_start:Ask about rescuing a nephew of an associate

$HeOrShe taps a datapad. "Mission logs point to $sep_sar_possibleLoc, and intercepted chatter suggests you'll need at least $sep_sar_marineAmount marines to hit their hideout."

script:

text:
The $manOrWoman glances you over, eyes briefly scanning your attire and ship insignia. After a quick tap at their datapad, they lean forward.

"My nephew, $sep_sar_survivorFullName, was captured during a survey mission and is now being held in a pirate hideout somewhere in $sep_sar_possibleLoc," $heOrShe says, voice tense.

"Intercepted chatter suggests, you'll need at least $sep_sar_marineAmount marines to storm the hideout, or you can pay the pirates $sep_sar_creditRansom."

$HeOrShe meets your gaze. "Just bring back my newphew and I'll reward you $sep_sar_creditReward."

Your $shipOrFleet approaches $entityName.

A preliminary scan detects a large underground outpost with multiple bio-signatures scattered inside it.

SAMPLE CONTACT OFFER TEXT

The channel flickers. The hard-eyed officer returns. "Payment confirmed. Credits received and cleared."

"We are dispatching a single shuttle carrying the hostage. It will intercept your position in approximately fifteen minutes. Keep your fleet at a safe distance. Any attempt to approach and we will shoot the shuttle down."

The shuttle appears on your sensors. A quick scan returns a single life sign aboard. You authorize the shuttle to dock and find $sep_sar_survivorFullName, alive but shaken.

You open comms and begin to persuade the kidnappers. After a tense back-and-forth, they finally relent. Their demands falter as hesitation takes hold, and they choose to release the hostage.

A lone shuttle is launched, carrying $sep_sar_survivorFullName toward your fleet. The hostage is alive but shaken, spared without a single shot fired.

The feed crackles back on. The kidnapper sneers, his voice dripping with disappointment. "So that's your choice? I thought you'd have the brains to settle this clean, but I see I was wrong. Fine, if it's a fight you want, then it's a fight you'll get."

Your marines advance into the pirate hideout with practiced precision, clearing the compound room by room. A few hostages are lost in the crossfire, but the assault is controlled and decisive, preventing further casualties.

During the operation, $sep_sar_survivorFullName, steps forward and directs your raid leader to the remaining survivors. They are secured and extracted back to your fleet for treatment.

Your sensors officer scans through the debris field, searching for any trace of the hostage. Among the wreckage, they discover a lone shuttle adrift.

Inside, $sep_sar_survivorFullName is found alive, shaken but stable, and quickly transferred to your sickbay for treatment.
SAMPLE SURVIVOR ALIVE TEXT
SAMPLE SURVIVOR DEAD TEXT

You report to $PersonPost $personLastName about the success of your mission, bringing the good news that $sep_sar_survivorFullName was found alive and well.

Afterward, you order your chief security officer to escort $sep_sar_survivorFullName to a safe location, while you provide a full report detailing the mission before receiving your reward.

The survivor appears on your screen, still recovering from the ordeal. "Thank you, Captain. If you hadn't reached me, I wouldn't be here."

$sep_sar_SurvivorHeOrShe pauses, choosing $sep_sar_survivorHisOrHer words. "I want to repay the favor and work for you. I need to file some paperwork, but once everything is settled, my contact info will be in your directory." 

$sep_sar_SurvivorHeOrShe thanks you once more before ending the call.

You report to $PersonPost $personLastName about the success of your mission, bringing the grim news that $sep_sar_survivorFullName had died by the time you reached $sep_sar_survivorHimOrHer.

Afterward, you order your chief security officer to escort the recovered remains to a safe location, while you provide a full report detailing the mission before receiving your reward.

You transmit the completed research data to the $personPost.

$HeOrShe thanks you for the data before sending you your reward.

You transmit the completed research data to the $personPost.

$HeOrShe thanks you for the data before getting lost in its details, even forgeting to cut the comm link.
"This one has been on our contract list for a while now, a risky but generous mission. It involves collecting real-time research data from black holes."

options: sep_srm_start:Ask about collecting real-time research data from black holes

text:
$PersonPost $PersonName pulls up a holographic display. "A researcher needs real-time data from inside a black hole's event horizon. They want a fleet to stay there for at least $sep_srm_dataInDays days, gathering live data the whole time."

$HeOrShe continues, "The payout is around $sep_srm_reward with bonuses and damage compensation included. There's also no deadline, so you can take your time."

$HeOrShe leans closer to the screen. "To tell you the truth, Captain, a fleet was already lost on this mission, so if you're still interested, just say the word."
A $personPost sits elegantly at the corner of the bar glancing at passing patrons till $heOrShe crosses eyes with you.

options: sep_srm_startBar:Introduce yourself to the $personPost sitting elegantly at the corner of the bar

text:
"Look, Captain, I've been rejected enough times to tell if someone will possibly say no. So I'll be direct," $heOrShe says.

"I need your fleet to go inside an event horizon." $heOrShe watches your reaction. "It's for my research about black holes. I need you to stay near one for at least $sep_srm_dataInDays days. Just enough time to gather all the data I need."

$HeOrShe adds, "You'll be compensated, of course. No hard deadline, either. Payout is around $sep_srm_reward, adjusted for damages and bonuses." $HeOrShe pauses and hands over a tripad. "What do you think?"
"A researcher needs your fleet to collect asteroid impact data. The requirement is simple, but the task is... peculiar. The question is whether that bothers you."

options: sep_srm_start:Ask about collecting asteroid impact data

text:
"A peculiar but simple task. The researcher wants your fleet to fly into some asteroids and gather the telemetry from your drive bubble at the point of impact. But don't worry, you'll be reimbursed for any damage sustained, on top of additional compensation." $heOrShe pauses and sends a documented contract to you.

On your tripad, the contract details are listed: an estimated reward of $sep_srm_reward, calculated from potential sustained damage and additional compensation; no written deadline; and a requirement to gather $sep_srm_dataInDays days' worth of asteroid impact data.

$HeOrShe clears their throat, drawing your attention back from the tripad. "So. Interested?"
A $manOrWoman in $personPost uniform is requesting help from passing captains but is largely rejected or ignored.

options: sep_srm_startBar:Talk to the $manOrWoman in $personPost uniform and ask what sort of help $heOrShe needs

text:
The $manOrWoman looks at you hesitantly. "I'll be blunt, Captain. I need your fleet to fly into some asteroids," $heOrShe says reluctantly. "I know how that sounds, but it's for my research, and you'll be more than fairly compensated."

$HeOrShe pauses and hands over a tripad with a contract. "As you can see, you'll be reimbursed for any damage sustained, on top of additional compensation, estimated to be around $sep_srm_reward," $heOrShe continues. "There's no deadline, either. I just need $sep_srm_dataInDays days' worth of asteroid impact data."

$HeOrShe glances down at the tripad, then back at you. "Interested?"
"A research contract was recently posted requiring the collection of telemetry from neutron stars, but due to the inherent risks, FleetComm has allowed third-party captains to accept."

options: sep_srm_start:Ask about collecting telemetry from neutron stars

text:
"The task is quite simple," $heOrShe explains. "Go to a neutron star, enter its rotating pulsar beam, and stay there for at least $sep_srm_dataInDays days. Just enough time to collect as much telemetry as we need." $HeOrShe pauses. "You Take as much time as you need. There's no deadline on this."

$HeOrShe continues, "Once the data is collected and sent, a credit reward of $sep_srm_reward will be issued. That includes compensation for any sustained damage during operations." $HeOrShe looks at you expectantly. "So, Captain. Do you think you're up for the task?"
A table filled with a research papers is being logged by $personPostAOrAn $personPost. A piece of paper falls from that table and slides next to your feet.

options: sep_srm_startBar:Pick the paper on your feet and approach the $personPost logging research papers

text:
You hand the paper back to the $personPost. The two of you exchange brief pleasantries, and after a moment, $heOrShe lays $hisOrHer tripad down to talk.

"I could use your help, Captain," $heOrShe says. "I want to complete my research on neutron stars, but I still need $sep_srm_dataInDays days' worth of live telemetry about pulsar beams." $heOrShe continues, "The public archives are insufficient, and anything important is locked behind clearance I don't have."

$HeOrShe pulls up a star chart on $hisOrHer tripad. "So the only thing I can do now is request someone to get it." $heOrShe slides the tripad toward you. "I know it's a risky job, but I'll compensate you for it, $sep_srm_reward to be precise. I don't mind if it takes a long time. Just get me the data, and the credits are yours." $heOrShe says while waiting for your reply.
"A researcher is throwing credits at anyone willing to fly out to the fringes of hyperspace and run sensor sweeps. Says they're looking for 'ghosts.'"

options: sep_srm_start:Ask about running sensor sweeps in hyperspace to look for "ghosts"

text:
"The researcher needs about $sep_srm_dataInDays days' worth of hyperspace sensor data. Specifically, and I quote, data about 'Hyperspace Ghosts,'" $heOrShe says skeptically. "Can't say much about this other than rumors of strange sensor readings when traveling deep in hyperspace."

$HeOrShe continues, transmitting the full mission briefing. "There's no set deadline for this assignment. Just gather the required data and send it directly to the researcher. Once done, you'll be awarded $sep_srm_reward. Interested?"
A lone $personPost sits in front of what looks like sensor logs while being ignored by fellow $personPosts.

options: sep_srm_startBar:Approach the lone $personPost sitting in front of what looks like sensor logs

text:
The $manOrWoman observes you before gesturing for you to sit. "Have you heard of sensor ghosts? Strange readings, impossible blips. Things that appear on sensors but aren't there." $HeOrShe pauses.

"I'm investigating them," $HeOrShe says. "My only issue now is that witness reports and official records don't seem to match." $heOrShe emphasizes, "So now I need a different way to get unobstructed data." $HeOrShe continues.

"That's where you come in, Captain," $heOrShe says with certainty. "I need live data on these ghosts. At least $sep_srm_dataInDays days' worth." $heOrShe adds, "I'll let you take your time. Just get me the data, and I'll pay you $sep_srm_reward." $HeOrShe leans forward. "Do we have a deal?"
"An associate of ours has requested for you personally, something about a research project invloving the Abyss"

options: sep_srm_start:Ask about the research project involving the Abyss

text:
$PersonPost $PersonName sends you the mission briefing before starting. "The associate wants to collect telemetry for their research project. They're offering $sep_srm_reward in exchange for $sep_srm_dataInDays days' worth of telemetry," $heOrShe pauses. "Telemetry only found in the Abyss."

"Due to the risks involved, the associate has placed no deadline for this mission. Just ensure that the data is collected and sent," $heOrShe adds. "And if you have any other questions, refer to the mission briefing." $HeOrShe asks, "What do you think, Captain?"

According to the mission briefing, the Abyss is the space between our galaxy's spirals, and reaching it requires going beyond this sector.
A message titled "Abyss Research" appears on your tripad. The sender asks to meet you in one of the bar's private rooms, $personPostAOrAn $personPost apparently.

options: sep_srm_startBar:Meet the sender of the "Abyss Research" message

text:
"It's finally nice to meet you, Captain. I'm $personName." $HeOrShe extends a hand for a handshake. You oblige and introduce yourself before $heOrShe continues.

"I've heard you're reliable, Captain. Someone who can do things outside standard purview." $heOrShe hands over a tripad. "We've been trying to collect data for our research project. A project involving the Abyss," $heOrShe emphasizes, "the seams between our galaxy's spirals."

"The task is simple. Go to the Abyss and gather $sep_srm_dataInDays days' worth of telemetry." $heOrShe adds, "Due to the risks and distance, there will be no deadline. In exchange, once the data is collected and received, you'll be paid $sep_srm_reward for your services." $HeOrShe looks at you expectantly. "So, Captain, do we have a deal?"
